success_factors: Workflow status is success with all expected nodes executed end-to-end (download, analysis, env, generate, run, review, finalize).
Import-based adapter strategy had high feasibility (0.92), reducing intrusive changes to source project.
Generated MCP plugin started healthy over stdio transport and passed plugin-level health checks.
Repository structure was analyzable via zip fallback and AST scan, enabling broad endpoint discovery.
failure_reasons: Quality gaps: original project tests were not executed/passing (reported passed=false with empty details), so runtime correctness of wrapped functions is unverified.
DeepWiki analysis failed, reducing external contextual enrichment.
Generated metadata appears incomplete/inconsistent (tool_endpoints=0 and total_lines_of_code=0 despite many discovered endpoints/files).

node_performance.download_time: Completed successfully; exact per-node timing unavailable. Repo imported via zip fallback (35 files), indicating robust but potentially slower fallback path.
node_performance.analysis_time: Completed successfully with extensive AST extraction across core modules; likely the dominant phase due to broad symbol discovery.
node_performance.generation_time: Completed successfully; 7 MCP output files created. Generation quality is functional but endpoint curation and metadata reporting need improvement.
node_performance.test_time: Plugin health check succeeded quickly; however, substantive functional/integration test execution is effectively missing for source and generated tool behavior.

resource_usage.memory_efficiency: Not measurable from provided metrics (reported 0). For a medium-complexity Python repo, usage is likely moderate.
resource_usage.cpu_efficiency: Not measurable from provided metrics (reported 0). AST-based analysis likely CPU-bound during symbol extraction.
resource_usage.disk_usage: Low-to-moderate; repo is small/medium (35 files) and plugin artifact footprint is limited, though file-size telemetry is missing.
